name: External Remote RDP Logon from Public IP
notes:
Converted from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Micah Babinski (@micahbabinski), Zach Mathis (@yamatosecurity)
SIGMA Title: External Remote RDP Logon from Public IP
SIGMA Description: Detects successful logon from public IP address via RDP. This can indicate a publicly-exposed RDP port.

False positives:
- Legitimate or intentional inbound connections from public IP addresses on the RDP port.

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event matches devicetype=12 AND ("Event ID"=4624 AND "Logon Type"=10 AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%-%') AQL filter query
when the source IP is one of the following 10.0.0.0/8, 127.0.0.0/8, 172.16.0.0/12, ::1/128, 192.168.0.0/16, 169.254.0.0/16, fc00::/7, fe80::/10
TD_list: APPLY External Remote RDP Logon from Public IP on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event matches devicetype=12 AND ("Event ID"=4624 AND "Logon Type"=10 AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%-%')  AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: External Remote RDP Logon from Public IP
